item.title: Okay, here's a thought...
item.content:encoded:
I *know* every SCP stands on it's own, and cross-linking is not needed nor directly *desired*.

All that said… What if the source of the 2532-As is SCP-1000? It's dead straight up their alley. And we were given "not forever".

But that requires thinking of the SCPs in an evolving world, which is not how they are written or meant to be taken. So just take it as an obscure possibility for someone to maybe stick into a Tale some day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would be interesting to see some more of these custom organisms.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Disembodied jaw structures which resemble enlarged versions of those found in Carcharodon Megalodon. Instances are capable of unaided propulsion through the air. Passage through the front of the jaws leads to a digestive tract, presumed to exist in extradimensional space. Instances prey primarily on Larus Canus.

Species names are lowercase.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel this would work better without the extradimensional mouths. It's not adding much, and doesn't mesh with the others; the rest are all things that could be non-anomalous but are too well adapted to the job, but the mouths are just bizarre.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You've got some interesting creatures, but it ultimately doesn't add up to anything. I was waiting for the other shoe to drop and it never did. People suggest a GoI connection but that seems like a cheap way out. Something that connects to the job offer thing, because right now it feels at odds with the creatures in the second half. Maybe they find humans filling in jobs in the world these creatures are from, perhaps observed by feeding a camera through the last entity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd considered them, but they seem to come from a (post?)apocalyptic alternate universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh, that might fit the best!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Light Courier?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It just feels ripe for Manna Charitable or Pangloss or something suggesting more than what's going on here. I dunno, I just wanted my mind to be blown after reading through it all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was more of an experiment in "can I write a good SCP using quirkyness alone" that trying to link it to a GoI or other org.

http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-1083394#post-2208113

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, this is pretty great. But I was kind of waiting for a zinger at the end, maybe some GoI link or something, so this feels slightly incomplete. I might upvote later, but I'd like to see it taken just one step farther.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, I am indeed dead.

Hush, you. You're dead. ;P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay! All better!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ultimately, I suspect you're getting hung up on a detail of minutiae that has no larger bearing of the rest of the article.

Sadly, I am like the scorpion in the fable. It is my nature.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed to "marsupialoid felines" since I feel that they're still definitely feline, but with marsupial traits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, I am indeed dead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Taxonomically, yeah, it probably is nonsense. But it's not presented as the actual taxonomic classification, it's a description of the beastie. Ultimately, I suspect you're getting hung up on a detail of minutiae that has no larger bearing of the rest of the article.

But, with the death of the author, everyone gets to interpret it differently. Que sera.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That works too. My objection here is grammatical (just the very specific grammar of taxonomy).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say they're more marsupialoid, if they're genetically compatible with felines.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't say "reptile" it says "reptilian". "Felinoid" would work fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think in this case "because anomalous" does pretty much cover it.
The idea of a feline that's re-evolved marsupialism is actually less strange than the six legged reptile. Marsupialism isn't that much of an overhaul (shorten gestation, add a pouch, done) but there are no six-limbed terrestrial chordate species, and there never have been.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am sorry, I am afraid that I am not being very clear. I don't have a problem with the anomalous interbreeding. My issue is with the phrase "marsupial feline". It cannot be meaningfully classified as both a metatherian and a member of family felidae because felidae is a member of the eutherian clade. The phrase seems to be taxonomically nonsensical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something can't have the characteristics of felines and marsupials anymore than say a vehicle can fit the specifications of a long haul tractor-trailer and also be a bicycle, because one of the characteristics of felines is a placenta. "Marsupial felinoids" might be better here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an anomalous animal that is somehow genetically compatible with common cats. Maybe that's part of the anomaly. Maybe they create a sterile hybrid offspring. The mechanics and genetics of it is outside the scope of the document, not necessary for containment and ultimately irrelevant to the story. "Marsupial feline" conjures a basic image to functionally describe one small aspect of the overall article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The way I see it, the Foundation is logging these things as marsupial felines because A) their young are born prematurely and move to the pouch until they grow older. And B) other than that, these things have the physical characteristics to get grouped with other felines. If something has the characteristics of felines and marsupials, I imagine that they would be classified as marsupial felines.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, I get that. My issue isn't that it is anomalous. My issue is that "marsupial feline" is biological nonsense. I don't even understand what that is supposed to mean. The other stuff is fine. "like a shark" isn't "is a shark" after all. When I asked for clarification "lots of cats can interbreed", while true isn't really helpful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And no one answering a help wanted ad could cause a heretofore unknown species to move into an area and integrate into the local ecosystem, either. Remember your MST3K Mantra.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And megalodons can't fly and have dimensional portals in their mouths.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All felines are placental mammals. No placental mammal could interbreed with a marsupial.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup. Believe it or not, quite a few different types of felines can interbreed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Marsupial "cats" that can interbreed with (placental) house cats?

item.title: New Scip.
item.content:encoded: Thank you to TyGently, LadyKatie, Rademacher, Vivax, TwistedGears, and MrWrong for reviewing this in chat.
